# Say Edge Cases Test
# Tests boundary conditions and special scenarios

# Build the say server binary
go build -o mcp-say-server .

# Test edge cases
exec mcp-say-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":"Unicode: 🚀 中文 émoji"}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":"Very long text that goes on and on and tests the limits of the text to speech system with a lot of words to see how it handles longer inputs"}},"id":4}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":"Numbers: 123 456 789"}},"id":5}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":"Special chars: !@#$%^&*()"}},"id":6}
EOF

# Verify unicode text handling
stdout 'Successfully spoke'
stdout 'Unicode: 🚀 中文 émoji'

# Verify long text handling
stdout 'Very long text'

# Verify numbers handling
stdout 'Numbers: 123 456 789'

# Verify special characters handling
stdout 'Special chars'
